title: V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything
rationale: The V51.1 physical/apparent sun altitude split creates a single coherent cascade failure across 12+ WINs. This is better presented as a unified cross-cutting argument than as scattered notes in individual WIN rebuttals. The argument has quantitative backbone: H(r) ceiling at sun orbit = 1,975-3,618 km (not 4,200), refraction needed = 13-27° (vs 0.03° real), n(r) inconsistency = 400-675x, seasonal angular diameter variation = 83%, tidal force modulation = 3-8x.
recommended_location: New subsection in Part 2 (Self-Contradictions) or as extension of Section 7.10/7.11 kill-shot analysis
outline: 1. The split: physical <4,200 km -> actually <1,975 km (ceiling at winter solstice orbit)
2. Refraction needed: 13-27 degrees varying seasonally (vs 0.03 degrees real atmospheric)
3. The n(r) contradiction: dome own formula gives ~0.04 degrees for stars, not 15+ degrees for the sun
4. Universal displacement: moon, stars, all triangulation baselines affected
5. Seasonal modulation: 83% angular diameter variation, 3-8x tidal force variation
6. Eclipse geometry paradox: shadows use physical positions, but physical altitude undefined
7. Self-defeating fix: the split was meant to resolve collision but creates model-wide inconsistency
